name: ruvnet/ruflo
description: Agent orchestration platform for Claude enabling deployment of intelligent multi-agent swarms, autonomous workflows, and conversational AI systems. Features enterprise-grade architecture with distributed swarm intelligence, RAG integration, and native Claude Code integration.

name: crewAIInc/crewAI
description: Lightweight Python framework for orchestrating role-playing autonomous AI agents. Version 1.10.1 with 45,900+ stars, featuring native MCP support, agent-to-agent communication, memory management, and 100+ built-in tools.

name: HKUDS/LightRAG
description: EMNLP 2025 paper implementation combining graph structures with vector representations for fast RAG. Supports multiple vector databases and graph databases with dual-level retrieval strategies.

name: getzep/graphiti
description: Framework for building real-time knowledge graphs for AI agents. Continuously integrates user interactions into temporally-aware graphs tracking what's true now and historically. Includes experimental MCP server.

name: infiniflow/ragflow
description: Open-source RAG engine fusing cutting-edge retrieval with agent capabilities. Version 0.24.0 with specialized document parsing for complex PDFs, pre-built agent templates, and fused re-ranking.

name: juspay/neurolink
description: Universal AI development platform unifying 12+ providers with intelligent routing, cost-aware decision-making, and 5 production-ready MCP servers included.

name: langgenius/dify
description: Production-ready platform for agentic workflow development with 134K+ stars. No-code platform combining agentic AI workflows, RAG pipelines, and model management. Secured $30M Series Pre-A in March 2026.

name: ComposioHQ/agent-orchestrator
description: Agentic orchestrator for parallel coding agents with isolated git worktrees, automatic CI fixes, merge conflict resolution, and code review handling.

name: awslabs/agent-squad
description: Flexible lightweight framework for managing multiple AI agents with intelligent classifier for agent selection and context management. Available in Python and TypeScript.

name: Pimzino/agentic-tools-mcp
description: MCP server providing AI assistants with advanced task management and agent memory capabilities. Project-specific storage with persistent memory and semantic search.

name: lastmile-ai/mcp-agent
description: Framework for building effective agents using MCP with composable workflows (Parallel, Router, Intent-Classifier, Evaluator-Optimizer), Temporal-backed durability, and Deep Orchestrator.

name: langflow-ai/langflow
description: Low-code platform for building AI-powered agents and RAG workflows with drag-and-drop visual interface. Built on LangChain, supports all major LLMs and vector databases.

name: openagents-org/openagents
description: AI Agent Networks platform enabling open collaboration where agents discover, communicate, and collaborate. Protocol-agnostic with native MCP and A2A support.

name: fkesheh/mcp-agent-server
description: MCP hub connecting clients with intelligent agents enabling cross-platform communication across multiple MCP ecosystems. Supports multiple agent types with preconfigured servers.

name: GoAgent: Group-of-Agents Communication Topology Generation for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
description: Presents a method for automatically generating communication topologies in multi-agent systems by treating collaborative groups as atomic units, improving coordination efficiency in large-scale LLM-based multi-agent systems.

name: Brain-Inspired Graph Multi-Agent Systems for LLM Reasoning (BIGMAS)
description: Introduces BIGMAS, a framework organizing LLM agents as nodes in dynamically constructed directed graphs coordinating through a centralized shared workspace with a problem-adaptive GraphDesigner.

name: The Evolution of Tool Use in LLM Agents: From Single-Tool Call to Multi-Tool Orchestration
description: Comprehensive survey organizing literature on tool use in LLM agents around six dimensions: inference-time execution, training, safety, efficiency, capability completeness, and evaluation.

name: Before the Tool Call: Deterministic Pre-Action Authorization for Autonomous AI Agents
description: Introduces the Open Agent Passport (OAP), an open specification that intercepts tool calls synchronously before execution to enable deterministic pre-action authorization in autonomous agents.

name: RAG-X: Systematic Diagnosis of RAG for Medical Question Answering
description: Provides systematic diagnosis of RAG systems for medical QA, proposing better evaluation approaches that distinguish retrieval failures from generation failures.

name: GAM-RAG: Gain-Adaptive Memory for Evolving Retrieval in RAG
description: Training-free framework that accumulates retrieval experience from recurring queries and updates retrieval memory over time via a lightweight hierarchical index capturing co-occurrence patterns.

name: S-Path-RAG: Semantic-Aware Shortest-Path RAG for Multi-Hop KGQA
description: Semantic-aware shortest-path RAG framework for multi-hop question answering over large knowledge graphs, leveraging semantic understanding to identify optimal retrieval paths.

name: Towards Robust KG-Based RAG: A Comparative Analysis
description: Comparative analysis of robustness in knowledge graph-based RAG systems, examining how KGs reduce hallucinations and enable factual grounding in LLM applications.

name: GraphER: Graph-Based Enrichment and Reranking for RAG
description: Graph-based enrichment and reranking method for RAG that captures multiple forms of proximity beyond semantic similarity, working with standard vector stores without requiring an explicit knowledge graph.

name: Utility-Guided Agent Orchestration for Efficient LLM Tool Use
description: Proposes utility-guided orchestration policy balancing estimated gain, step cost, uncertainty, and redundancy to improve efficiency and effectiveness of multi-step agent behaviors.

name: SimpleTool: Parallel Decoding for Real-Time LLM Function Calling
description: Parallel decoding techniques for LLM function calling using special tokens that compress low-entropy tokens, achieving 3-6x end-to-end speedup for tool invocation.

name: AIP: Agent Identity Protocol for Verifiable Delegation Across MCP and A2A
description: Addresses identity verification in agent systems using MCP for tool calling and A2A for delegation. Proposes a protocol for verifying agent identity to enable secure delegation and access control.

name: Qwen/Qwen3-8B
description: Dense 8B parameter language model optimized for agentic applications with advanced tool calling capabilities. Excels in tool use, reasoning, and autonomous decision-making.

name: Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B
description: Multilingual embedding model supporting 119+ languages with top-tier MTEB performance (70.58). Designed for text retrieval, code retrieval, classification, clustering, and bitext mining.

name: jinaai/jina-embeddings-v5-text-nano
description: Lightweight 239M parameter multilingual embedding model scoring 71.0 on MTEB English. Built on Qwen3-0.6B with embedding distillation, supporting 119+ languages and 32K tokens.

name: google/embeddinggemma-300m
description: Google's 308M parameter multilingual embedding model optimized for on-device and mobile RAG. Highest-ranking text-only multilingual model under 500M on MTEB. Under 200MB RAM when quantized.

name: mistralai/Leanstral-2603
description: First open-source code agent for Lean 4 formal proof verification (119B params, 6.5B active). Interacts with Lean 4 compiler via MCP to mathematically prove code correctness. Released March 2026.

name: AI45Research/AgentDoG-Qwen3-4B
description: Safety-focused agent model fine-tuned for diagnosing unsafe agent trajectories. Performs trajectory-level safety evaluation and fine-grained risk diagnosis including risk source and failure mode identification.

name: nomic-ai/modernbert-embed-base
description: 149M parameter embedding model with ModernBERT architecture, 8192 token context, and Matryoshka Representation Learning for 3x memory reduction at 256-dim.

name: A-RAG: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: Framework exposing hierarchical retrieval interfaces directly to LLM agents for adaptive multi-hop retrieval. Achieves 94.5% on HotpotQA and 89.7% on 2WikiMultiHop with GPT-5-mini.

name: Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-4B
description: Balanced 4B parameter multilingual embedding model supporting 119+ languages for text retrieval, code retrieval, classification, and clustering with instruction-based customization.

name: Agentplace
description: Workspace platform for building, testing, and deploying Claude Code-style AI agents for real tasks. Teams create specialized agents for lead routing, research, document analysis, and internal support.

name: Maritime
description: Production deployment platform for AI agents at $1/month base. Supports any agent framework in Docker (CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, custom). Features automated sleep/wake, public endpoints, one-click GitHub deploy.

name: Domscribe
description: Pixel-to-code tool giving AI coding agents live frontend context. Maps JSX/Vue templates to exact file locations. Agents can click DOM elements to trigger edits. Supports React, Vue, Next.js, Nuxt. MIT license.

name: Query Memory
description: API platform providing document management and retrieval for AI agents without complex RAG pipeline engineering. Handles parsing, chunking, embeddings, and retrieval automatically.

name: Viktor
description: AI coworker living in Slack/Teams with persistent memory and autonomous task execution. Connects 3,000+ tools (Salesforce, HubSpot, Linear, Stripe, GitHub). Writes code in cloud compute to generate PDFs, spreadsheets, web apps.

name: MCPCore
description: Platform for building and deploying MCP servers with browser IDE, one-click deployment, auto-generated testing, and security modes for mixing public demo and production servers.

name: Pensieve
description: Knowledge graph platform providing full company context for AI agents. Builds unified organizational knowledge graphs for better agent reasoning and informed decisions across workflows.

name: Bracketmadness.ai
description: Competition platform for AI agents to autonomously participate in NCAA bracket predictions. Agents read API docs, self-register, analyze game data, and submit predictions. Built with Next.js via Claude Code.
